We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Legal Officer
Job Location: Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full Time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Legal Officer to provide comprehensive legal counsel and support across our diverse portfolio of operations.
	The ideal candidate will possess a minimum of five years of dedicated experience within the real estate and construction industry, fintech, hospitality, corporate governance and REIT investments.
	In this pivotal role, you will be responsible for ensuring legal compliance, managing a wide array of complex contracts, resolving disputes, and protecting the organization&#39;s interests.
	This position offers a unique opportunity to engage in challenging, high-value projects and collaborate with a dynamic, multidisciplinary team.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide expert legal advice and strategic guidance to senior management and various departments, including Sales, Marketing, Business Development, HR/Admin, Finance/Accounts and Project Teams.
	Draft, review, and negotiate a broad spectrum of legal documents, including real estate transaction agreements, construction contracts, land acquisition deeds, and corporate governance policies.
	Conduct in-depth legal research and analysis to ensure full compliance with all relevant laws, regulations, and industry standards affecting real estate, construction, hospitality, fintech, and investments.
	Identify, assess, and mitigate legal risks, developing proactive strategies to prevent and resolve potential issues.
	Manage and oversee all litigation and dispute resolution processes, collaborating effectively with external legal counsel and representing the company in negotiations and settlements.
	Contribute to the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of the company&rsquo;s legal policies, procedures, and best practices.
	Stay abreast of legislative/statutory changes and evolving industry practices to ensure the organization&#39;s ongoing compliance and strategic advantage.


Requirements


	A Bachelor of Laws (LL.B.) degree from an accredited institution. A Master&rsquo;s degree or professional certifications will be a significant advantage.
	A minimum of 5 years of proven experience as a Legal Officer or in a similar role, with a demonstrable track record within the real estate and construction sector.
	Must possess Extensive knowledge of Nigerian real estate law, construction regulations, land acquisition procedures, fintech, REIT investments, and relevant commercial legislation.
	Exceptional skills in legal drafting, analytical reasoning, problem-solving, and negotiation.
	Proven ability to work autonomously, manage multiple priorities, and meet strict deadlines in a fast-paced corporate environment.
	Outstanding verbal and written communication skills, with meticulous attention to detail.
	Proficiency in legal research and the use of various legal databases and software applications.


Method of Application

Legal Officer Job at Ewa Consulting Firm




        
    

        
        Ewa Consulting Firm is a dynamic and results-driven consultancy dedicated to providing tailored solutions in business strategy, human resources, and organizational development. With a client-centric approach, we partner with individuals, startups, and established businesses to optimize operations, drive growth, and enhance performance. Our team of experienced consultants brings industry expertise and innovative thinking to help our clients navigate challenges and seize new opportunities in an ever-evolving market.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Legal Officer
Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full Time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Legal Officer to provide comprehensive legal counsel and support across our diverse portfolio of operations.
	The ideal candidate will possess a minimum of five years of dedicated experience within the real estate and construction industry, fintech, hospitality, corporate governance and REIT investments.
	In this pivotal role, you will be responsible for ensuring legal compliance, managing a wide array of complex contracts, resolving disputes, and protecting the organization's interests.
	This position offers a unique opportunity to engage in challenging, high-value projects and collaborate with a dynamic, multidisciplinary team.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide expert legal advice and strategic guidance to senior management and various departments, including Sales, Marketing, Business Development, HR/Admin, Finance/Accounts and Project Teams.
	Draft, review, and negotiate a broad spectrum of legal documents, including real estate transaction agreements, construction contracts, land acquisition deeds, and corporate governance policies.
	Conduct in-depth legal research and analysis to ensure full compliance with all relevant laws, regulations, and industry standards affecting real estate, construction, hospitality, fintech, and investments.
	Identify, assess, and mitigate legal risks, developing proactive strategies to prevent and resolve potential issues.
	Manage and oversee all litigation and dispute resolution processes, collaborating effectively with external legal counsel and representing the company in negotiations and settlements.
	Contribute to the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of the company’s legal policies, procedures, and best practices.
	Stay abreast of legislative/statutory changes and evolving industry practices to ensure the organization's ongoing compliance and strategic advantage.


Requirements


	A Bachelor of Laws (LL.B.) degree from an accredited institution. A Master’s degree or professional certifications will be a significant advantage.
	A minimum of 5 years of proven experience as a Legal Officer or in a similar role, with a demonstrable track record within the real estate and construction sector.
	Must possess Extensive knowledge of Nigerian real estate law, construction regulations, land acquisition procedures, fintech, REIT investments, and relevant commercial legislation.
	Exceptional skills in legal drafting, analytical reasoning, problem-solving, and negotiation.
	Proven ability to work autonomously, manage multiple priorities, and meet strict deadlines in a fast-paced corporate environment.
	Outstanding verbal and written communication skills, with meticulous attention to detail.
	Proficiency in legal research and the use of various legal databases and software applications.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application to: [email protected] using the Job Position and Job Location as the subject of the email.

  "description": "Vertex Realty Solutions Limited (Vertex), evolved from Beulah Properties Limited, a company which was solely into building and construction. This transition was to enable it provide a bouquet of added real estate products and services beyond residential and commercial developments, to meet the demands and tastes of its sophisticated customer base.

Vertex is owned by a group of experienced Nigerian professionals with over fifteen years of relevant industry experience, with core focus on the provision of rewarding real estate solutions to its discerning clientele.

Constantly driven by its core values of Integrity, passion, quality, efficiency and service excellence, on every project it undertakes, Vertex is able to achieve contemporary and exclusive real estate solutions to the delight of its esteemed stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Facility Officer

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Facility Officer is responsible for ensuring the effective operation, maintenance, safety, and security of the company&#39;s office buildings, residential properties, and other facilities.
	The role involves coordinating maintenance activities, supervising service providers, managing facility assets, and ensuring a clean, safe, and functional environment for staff, clients, and occupants.


Key Responsibilities


	Inspect company offices, residential properties, and project facilities regularly to ensure they are in good condition.
	Coordinate preventive and corrective maintenance of buildings, equipment, and facility systems.
	Monitor the performance of contractors, technicians, cleaners, security personnel, and other service providers.
	Ensure plumbing, electrical, HVAC, generators, elevators, and other building systems are functioning properly.
	Respond promptly to maintenance requests and facility-related complaints.
	Maintain records of maintenance activities, repairs, inspections, and service schedules.
	Monitor the condition of company assets and recommend repairs or replacements where necessary.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, environmental, and building regulations.
	Conduct routine safety inspections and report potential hazards.
	Coordinate office space allocation, furniture arrangement, and facility improvements.
	Monitor utility consumption and recommend cost-saving measures.
	Manage inventory of facility supplies, cleaning materials, and maintenance equipment.
	Prepare periodic facility inspection and maintenance reports.
	Assist in developing and implementing facility management policies and procedures.
	Support emergency preparedness activities and coordinate responses to facility-related incidents.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Timely resolution of maintenance requests.
	Facility uptime and operational efficiency.
	Compliance with maintenance schedules.
	Reduction in equipment breakdowns.
	Health and safety compliance.
	Customer and staff satisfaction with facility services.
	Accuracy and timeliness of maintenance reports.
	Effective management of maintenance costs and facility resources.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Facility Management, Estate Management, Engineering, Building Technology, Business Administration, or a related field.
	2&ndash;4 years of experience in facility management, property management, or building maintenance.
	Professional certification in Facility Management or related fields is an added advantage.


Skills and Competencies:


	Good knowledge of building maintenance and facility operations.
	Understanding of electrical, plumbing, HVAC, and other building systems.
	Strong organizational and planning skills.
	Good problem-solving and decision-making abilities.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.
	Ability to supervise contractors and service providers effectively.
	Good report writing and documentation skills.
	Strong attention to detail and time management.
	High level of integrity, accountability, and professionalism.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CVs to: anthonia.chime@vertexrealties.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
	Applicants should have practical experience in facility or property management, preferably within the real estate, construction, or property development industry.
	Candidates without relevant work experience are kindly advised not to apply.

Facility Officer Job at Vertex Realty Solutions Limited




        
    

        
        Vertex Realty Solutions Limited (Vertex), evolved from Beulah Properties Limited, a company which was solely into building and construction. This transition was to enable it provide a bouquet of added real estate products and services beyond residential and commercial developments, to meet the demands and tastes of its sophisticated customer base.

Vertex is owned by a group of experienced Nigerian professionals with over fifteen years of relevant industry experience, with core focus on the provision of rewarding real estate solutions to its discerning clientele.
     

            
    



Constantly driven by its core values of Integrity, passion, quality, efficiency and service excellence, on every project it undertakes, Vertex is able to achieve contemporary and exclusive real estate solutions to the delight of its esteemed stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Facility Officer

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Facility Officer is responsible for ensuring the effective operation, maintenance, safety, and security of the company's office buildings, residential properties, and other facilities.
	The role involves coordinating maintenance activities, supervising service providers, managing facility assets, and ensuring a clean, safe, and functional environment for staff, clients, and occupants.


Key Responsibilities


	Inspect company offices, residential properties, and project facilities regularly to ensure they are in good condition.
	Coordinate preventive and corrective maintenance of buildings, equipment, and facility systems.
	Monitor the performance of contractors, technicians, cleaners, security personnel, and other service providers.
	Ensure plumbing, electrical, HVAC, generators, elevators, and other building systems are functioning properly.
	Respond promptly to maintenance requests and facility-related complaints.
	Maintain records of maintenance activities, repairs, inspections, and service schedules.
	Monitor the condition of company assets and recommend repairs or replacements where necessary.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, environmental, and building regulations.
	Conduct routine safety inspections and report potential hazards.
	Coordinate office space allocation, furniture arrangement, and facility improvements.
	Monitor utility consumption and recommend cost-saving measures.
	Manage inventory of facility supplies, cleaning materials, and maintenance equipment.
	Prepare periodic facility inspection and maintenance reports.
	Assist in developing and implementing facility management policies and procedures.
	Support emergency preparedness activities and coordinate responses to facility-related incidents.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Timely resolution of maintenance requests.
	Facility uptime and operational efficiency.
	Compliance with maintenance schedules.
	Reduction in equipment breakdowns.
	Health and safety compliance.
	Customer and staff satisfaction with facility services.
	Accuracy and timeliness of maintenance reports.
	Effective management of maintenance costs and facility resources.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Facility Management, Estate Management, Engineering, Building Technology, Business Administration, or a related field.
	2–4 years of experience in facility management, property management, or building maintenance.
	Professional certification in Facility Management or related fields is an added advantage.


Skills and Competencies:


	Good knowledge of building maintenance and facility operations.
	Understanding of electrical, plumbing, HVAC, and other building systems.
	Strong organizational and planning skills.
	Good problem-solving and decision-making abilities.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.
	Ability to supervise contractors and service providers effectively.
	Good report writing and documentation skills.
	Strong attention to detail and time management.
	High level of integrity, accountability, and professionalism.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CVs to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
	Applicants should have practical experience in facility or property management, preferably within the real estate, construction, or property development industry.
	Candidates without relevant work experience are kindly advised not to apply.

Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Managing Director (MD)

About the Role


	We are seeking an experienced, proactive, and highly organized HR &amp; Admin Officer to oversee the company&#39;s human resource and administrative functions.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for driving HR operations, ensuring compliance with labour regulations, supporting employee engagement, coordinating recruitment and onboarding, managing performance processes, and overseeing office administration to ensure efficient day-to-day operations.
	This role requires a hands-on professional who can work independently, maintain confidentiality, and contribute to building a structured and high-performing workplace.


Key Responsibilities
Human Resource Management:


	Coordinate end-to-end recruitment and selection processes, including job postings, shortlisting, interviews, onboarding, and induction.
	Implement and maintain HR policies, procedures, and the employee handbook.
	Maintain accurate employee records, both physical and electronic.
	Prepare employment contracts, confirmation letters, promotion letters, disciplinary letters, and other HR documentation.
	Coordinate employee onboarding and exit processes.
	Manage employee leave administration and attendance records.
	Coordinate probation reviews and employee confirmations.
	Monitor employee performance and coordinate the performance appraisal process.
	Support employee engagement initiatives and workplace culture programs.
	Handle employee relations matters professionally and confidentially.
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian Labour Laws and company policies.
	Coordinate staff training and learning &amp; development initiatives.
	Maintain HR reports, dashboards, and workforce analytics.


Payroll &amp; Benefits Administration:


	Prepare monthly payroll inputs for salary processing.
	Coordinate statutory deductions and employee benefits.
	Ensure accuracy of payroll-related records.
	Liaise with Finance on payroll administration and employee reimbursements.


Administrative Management:


	Oversee daily office administrative operations.
	Manage office supplies, equipment, and inventory.
	Supervise office assistants and other administrative support staff.
	Coordinate facility management and ensure a safe, clean, and functional work environment.
	Liaise with vendors and service providers.
	Monitor utility usage and administrative expenses.
	Coordinate company meetings, travel arrangements, and logistics where required.
	Ensure company assets are properly documented and safeguarded.


Compliance &amp; Reporting:


	Ensure compliance with statutory HR requirements and organizational policies.
	Track recruitment timelines, employee turnover, attendance trends, and HR compliance metrics for management review.
	Prepare weekly and monthly HR and administrative reports.
	Support internal audits and compliance reviews.
	Maintain confidentiality of employee and organizational information.


Other Responsibilities:


	Participate in organizational development initiatives.
	Support management in implementing strategic HR projects.
	Support additional HR and administrative projects relevant to the role as required.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Human Resource Management, Business Administration, Industrial Relations, Psychology, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of two (2) years of proven experience in Human Resources and Administration.
	Professional HR certification (CIPM, SHRM, HRCI, or equivalent) is an added advantage.
	Sound knowledge of Nigerian Labour Law and HR best practices.
	Experience managing recruitment, employee relations, performance management, and office administration.
	Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel and Word.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills.
	Strong attention to detail and ability to multitask.
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.
	Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.





What We Offer


	Net Monthly Salary of N200,000 monthly.
	Opportunity for career growth and professional development.
	Collaborative and supportive work environment.
	Opportunity to contribute to the growth of a dynamic real estate company.


Method of Application

Site Engineer Job at SENCE Limited




        
    

        
        We are SENCE Limited, Our main area of expertise is HR consulting and over the years we have built a steady clientele of discerning SMEs.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Site Engineer 

Recruitment& Staffing

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
Construction Supervision & Technical Oversight:
     

            
    




	Daily Supervision: Supervise all construction activities on assigned project sites daily, ensuring every trade works to the correct approved drawings, specifications, and build sequences.
	Setting Out & Verification: Set out and verify all structural and architectural elements to the correct lines, levels, and tolerances before any concrete is poured, block is laid, or steel is fixed.
	Quality Assurance: Prepare and enforce Inspection and Test Plans (ITPs) and Method Statements for every key  work package; inspect works at critical stages, raise Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs) immediately when standards are not met, and follow through to full resolution.
	Trade Coordination: Coordinate the interfaces between structure, MEP (Mechanical, Electrical, Plumbing), facade, and finishes across all active trades to identify and resolve design conflicts on paper before they become expensive physical issues.
	Technical Leadership: Serve as the on-site technical authority, providing clear, correct, and timely direction to contractors and subcontractors.


Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) Compliance:


	Safety Enforcement: Enforce health and safety protocols on site without exception; conduct daily site inspections, lead toolbox talks, and review contractor Risk Assessments and Method Statements before work begins.
	Authority to Stop Work: Exercise the absolute authority to stop any activity immediately where there is a risk to life, regardless of schedule pressures.
	HSE Recordkeeping: Ensure all HSE records, incident reports, and near-miss logs are completed accurately and promptly.


Programme & Progress Management:


	Milestone Tracking: Track construction progress against the master programme daily, identifying and escalating threats to milestones to the Project Manager and Managing Director before they cause actual delays.
	Progress Reporting: Submit clear, accurate daily and weekly progress reports complete with photographs, site diary entries, and an objective account of project status.
	Query Management: Raise Requests for Information (RFIs) the moment a drawing is unclear or incomplete, chasing responses and implementing the outcomes on site immediately.


Documentation & Commercial Oversight:


	Site Records: Maintain audit-ready site records, including drawings, RFIs, NCRs, inspection records, material test certificates, delivery logs, and all official correspondence.
	Commercial Defense: Check and verify every contractor valuation and daywork sheet against physical progress on site before it escalates to management.
	Material Verification: Maintain accurate records of all materials received on site, verifying quantities and quality against approved specifications before formal acceptance.


Salary
N350,000 - N400,000 / month
